chunk_summary: This section discusses dietary diversity and meal frequency indicators for assessing micronutrient adequacy and feeding practices in children aged 6–23 months, emphasizing their use as screening tools and programmatic applications in India.
learning_objectives: Understand the WHO minimum dietary diversity (MDD) score and its food group criteria.
Learn how minimum meal frequency (MMF) and minimum acceptable diet (MAD) integrate diversity and feeding frequency.
Explore clinical and programmatic applications of dietary indicators, including counseling strategies and monitoring caregiver behavior change.
key_facts: WHO MDD score requires consumption of at least 5 out of 8 food groups in the previous day.
Indicators are validated primarily for children aged 6–23 months; older children require qualitative assessment.
India's NFHS-5 reports low coverage of MDD and MAD indicators.
Updated frameworks track risk patterns like 'zero fruit or vegetable' and 'unhealthy food consumption.'
Counseling focuses on adding missing food groups (e.g., pulses, eggs, fruits/vegetables) and reducing unhealthy intake.
reference_quote: The WHO minimum dietary diversity (MDD) score counts whether the child consumed at least 5 of 8 food groups in the previous day.
chunk_heading: DIETARY ASSESSMENT & FEEDING SKILLS EVALUATION;DIETARY DIVERSITY AND MEAL FREQUENCY INDICATORS;
subtopic: DIETARY DIVERSITY AND MEAL FREQUENCY INDICATORS
